channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Tesla Model Y is first car to meet new U.S. driver assistance safety benchmark
item.description: The new benchmark rating applies to 2026 Tesla Model Y vehicles assembled on or after November 12, 2025.

item.title: Your next Samsung Galaxy Watch could ‘dramatically reduce’ your chances of injury thanks to this one clever feature
item.description: Future smartwatches could predict fainting episodes and reduce the chances of injuries before they happen.

item.title: Perplexity’s Personal Computer is now available everyone on Mac
item.description: Perplexity's Personal Computer brings AI agents to your Mac, and is now open to everyone.

item.title: Mira Murati’s deposition pulled back the curtain on Sam Altman’s ouster
item.description: The week leading up to Thanksgiving 2023 was the AI industry's biggest soap opera moment. OpenAI CEO Sam Altman was abruptly ousted from his role at the ChatGPT-maker. The explanation? That Altman was "not consistently candid in his communications with the board." Now, via witness testimony and trial exhibits in Musk v. Altman, the public […]

item.title: The Lexus TZ is a quieter, upscale take on the Highlander EV
item.description: It's also the company's first fully-electric three-row SUV.

item.title: Why high-growth companies should build decision cultures
item.description: As AI accelerates the pace of business, organizations need to move decisions closer to those with intimate knowledge. At the Exceptional Women Alliance, we help senior women leaders mentor one another through shared insight. As founder, chair, and CEO, I speak with executives shaping how organizations evolve and perform.

item.title: Apple’s AirPods with cameras for AI are apparently close to production
item.description: Apple's rumored AirPods with cameras are nearing a stage where the company will test early mass production, Bloomberg's Mark Gurman reports. Currently, Apple testers are "actively using" prototypes that are in the design validation test stage, which is one step before the production validation test stage. The AirPods' cameras "aren't designed" to snap photos or […]...

item.title: Why the Hantavirus Cruise Ship Outbreak Isn’t Likely to Become a Global Crisis
item.description: While the outbreak aboard a cruise ship in the Atlantic is concerning, the virus isn’t easily transmitted through casual contact.

item.title: Amazon has a massive Fitbit sale ahead of its newest release —shop the best deals from $79.95
item.description: The all-new Google Fitbit Air is now available to preorder, so you can find incredible deals on top-rated Fitbit activity and smartwatches, with prices starting at $79.95.

item.title: I'm already dreading Apple's camera-equipped AirPods
item.description: Must everything be part of the panopticon?

item.title: ‘I’m selling stuff on eBay to pay for eBay,’ GameStop CEO Ryan Cohen posts following combative interview
item.description: After offering to buy eBay, the billionaire CEO avoided questions on CNBC this week about how the company would fill its $16 billion gap in funding. GameStop announced on Sunday that it would offer to buy eBay for nearly $56 billion. One day later, CNBC spoke to GameStop CEO Ryan Cohen about the news, in an interview media outlets have called “bizarre,” “evasive,” “dizzying” and “awkward.” ...

item.title: Here’s who AI picked to win the 2026 World Cup. It’s not who FIFA fans think
item.description: According to a new Bank of America survey, 40% of fans think France’s Les Bleus will take home the trophy—but AI begs to differ. Artificial inteligence is being touted as the most transformative technology of the 21st century, changing everything from how people work to how they live. But forget all that—what sports fans want to know is, can it predict who is going to win the World Cup?...

item.title: SpaceX has a $55 billion plan to build AI chips in Texas
item.description: Elon Musk's plans to get into the AI chip manufacturing business are going to be costly. As the New York Times and CNBC report, SpaceX is planning to invest at least $55 billion into its "Terafab" chip plant in Austin, Texas. That's according to the details of a public hearing notice filed in Grimes County, […]

item.title: Perplexity opens up its Personal Computer AI assistant to all Mac users
item.description: It's no longer locked a Max subscription.

item.title: Elon Musk’s lawsuit is putting OpenAI’s safety record under the microscope
item.description: Elon Musk's legal effort to dismantle OpenAI may hinge on how its for-profit subsidiary enhances or detracts from the frontier lab's founding mission of ensuring that humanity benefits from artificial general intelligence.

item.title: The fastest-growing target in America’s book ban wave might surprise you
item.description: A new report says nonfiction titles about identity, activism, and health are being banned from schools at rapidly increasing rates. Book bans are on the rise—and they’re increasingly focused on censoring facts.

item.title: Bumble is getting rid of the swipe, CEO says
item.description: Based on Whitney Wolfe Herd's past comments about Bumble's new direction, the company is expected to lean into AI -- Bumble is even working on an AI dating assistant called Bee, and the CEO has made many comments over the years about how AI will be "a supercharger to love and relationships."

item.title: Peak Design is taking up to 50 percent off some of our favorite bags right now
item.description: To celebrate founder Peter Dering’s birthday, Peak Design is taking up to 50 percent off a host of bags and accessories through May 8. The sale includes steep discounts on many of the company’s most popular backpacks and bags, as well as a couple of other items, including camera accessories. Many Verge staffers are fans […]

item.title: 'Like a microscopic Predator': Chinese scientists create tiny robotic vacuum to hunt radioactive pollution and clean the world's oceans
item.description: Chinese scientists created light-driven micromotors that actively capture uranium from water, offering a new approach to resource extraction challenges.

item.title: Tome, another Goodreads book-tracker rival, shuts down
item.description: Built on the back of the sizable and influential BookTok community -- creators who discuss and review books on TikTok -- the app offered readers a place to chronicle and rate their books, get recommendations, and even add photos of things like favorite quotes or memes, or share playlists that match the book's vibes.

item.title: Could Lovable’s automatic 10% pay raise be the cure for toxic cultures?
item.description: Stockholm-based vibe-coding platform Lovable is offering employees an automatic 10% raise as a way to sidestep classic corporate politics.

item.title: Amazon Luna's May lineup includes Guardians of the Galaxy and the Resident Evil 2 remake
item.description: Prime members can also claim a code for Mafia II: Definitive Edition.

item.title: How I Fixed My Webcam Lighting for Zoom Calls (2026)
item.description: I have a horrendous lighting setup in my home office, but as a mixed-use room, my options for fixing it were limited.

item.title: Hackers hack victims hacked by other hackers
item.description: An unknown group of hackers is breaking into systems previously breached by the cybercrime group TeamPCP. Once inside, the hackers immediately kick out TeamPCP and remove its hacking tools from the victims’ systems.

item.title: Valve will open reservations on May 8 for the second wave of Steam Controllers
item.description: Hopefully fewer of the coveted controllers will be snapped up by bots and resellers this time.
